_doc: The defect->lesson ledger. Each lesson: a defect class the grader found, its root cause, the builder rule that fixes it, and (where possible) an automated GUARD that checks the invariant against build artifacts BEFORE deploy. lessons.mjs --audit runs the guards (free, pre-deploy regression catcher); --classify maps a fresh grade's defects to lessons (RECURRENCE vs NEW). Goal: every defect class is fixed once and never recurs, so build cycles shrink.

lessons.title: Gradient-clipped / -webkit-text-fill text color lies (the green headline)
lessons.root_cause: getComputedStyle().color returns the gradient / -webkit-text-fill FALLBACK, not the painted color, so navy headlines record as rgb(129,184,26) green.
lessons.builder_rule: capture-fx samples the REAL painted color from a dpr-2 screenshot (edge-aware + glyph-core extraction). Never trust computed color for clipped text.
lessons.note: VERIFIED LIVE on page 805: headline gc-0 color=rgb(2,8,38) navy, green gone. Proxy≠reality gap closed.

lessons.title: Pill buttons lose their fill (missing purple CTA)
lessons.root_cause: build-ir-elementor emitted every button as a bare text-editor <a>, discarding the captured background, radius and text color.
lessons.builder_rule: A captured button with an opaque background becomes a native button widget carrying background_color + button_text_color + border_radius.
lessons.note: Fixed in builder (audit guard passes). A live grade of a not-yet-redeployed page will still show this — STALE-DEPLOY signal, not a code regression.

lessons.title: Full-bleed image rendered as a foreground widget (the nav black box)
lessons.root_cause: A full-bleed decorative/hero image emitted as a foreground image widget renders as a broken/black box and over-dominates the layout.
lessons.builder_rule: Images spanning >=70% of viewport width become cover background containers, not foreground image widgets.

lessons.title: Gradient TEXT must keep its gradient (not the fallback)
lessons.root_cause: Gradient text (-webkit-background-clip:text) reads a solid fallback color from getComputedStyle.
lessons.builder_rule: capture-fx detects gradient-text and stores the real gradient; build reapplies it via background-clip CSS.

lessons.title: Elementor drops _css_classes on CONTAINERS
lessons.root_cause: Elementor strips _css_classes from container elements, so class-based positioning/styling on containers silently fails.
lessons.builder_rule: Use _element_id + #id selectors for container-level styling, never _css_classes on containers.

lessons.title: Vertical-rhythm / height inflation (~1.6x)
lessons.root_cause: Implicit line-height + re-derived geometry inflate text block height.
lessons.builder_rule: Emit explicit captured line-height; use faithful absolute geometry, never re-derive.

candidates.title: Missing SEO meta (title / description / OG / structured data)
candidates.root_cause: The builder sets no page title/description/social meta; WP defaults leak ('… – My WordPress').
candidates.builder_rule: Carry the source's title + meta description + canonical + OG/Twitter tags onto the page (page_settings / post meta); preserve JSON-LD if present; exactly one h1.
candidates.note: PARTIAL (guard correctly regressed it): page title carried, but seo guard fails on still-missing meta description + OG + JSON-LD → needs head-injection (SEO plugin / wp_head), not deployable via Elementor content. Not fixed.

candidates.title: Effect vocabulary dropped (shadows / radius / blur / gradients)
candidates.root_cause: DIAGNOSED: the IR style-class signature carries effects (shadow/filter/transform — 5 box-shadow rules DO reach live) but NOT border-radius (non-button) or BACKGROUND GRADIENTS, so radius 200→13 and gradient 18→0. Those props arent threaded from capture into the styleClass.
candidates.builder_rule: Thread el.radius + el.bgImage(gradient) into build-ir styleSig and emit them in emitStyleClasses (border-radius + background gradient on the widget container).

candidates.title: Clone uses placeholder / broken images instead of real ones
candidates.root_cause: The builder hotlinks placehold.co or drops images; real captured imagery isn't uploaded to WP media.
candidates.builder_rule: Download captured images to WP media and reference those URLs; never ship placehold.co in a fidelity build.
candidates.note: VERIFIED LIVE: 10 real images, 0 broken, same-origin; 1 placeholder remains (minor). imagery dim 0.909.

candidates.title: Mixed content: https page loads http assets (console errors, blocked/insecure)
candidates.root_cause: Clone references assets over http (or a CDN that 301s) on an https page → browser blocks/warns; silent breakage the visual grader misses.
candidates.builder_rule: All asset URLs must be https; prefer uploading to WP media (same-origin https) over hotlinking external CDNs.
candidates.note: NOT our defect: live page has no real http resource loads (only w3.org SVG xmlns). The 12 console errors are theme/host JS, not clone content. Lower priority; re-scope as environmental.

candidates.title: Clone LCP far slower than source (load speed)
candidates.root_cause: Hotlinked external images (slow cross-origin fetch) + Elementor/theme overhead + slow TTFB inflate Largest Contentful Paint.
candidates.builder_rule: Download images to WP media (served same-origin, cacheable); set explicit width/height to avoid reflow; lazy-load below-fold; keep injected CSS lean. Target LCP < 2.5s and within ~1.25x of source.
candidates.note: FIX FAILED on live: sized large bg did NOT lower LCP (5724→6992ms, noisy/worse). LCP is partly host-bound (SiteGround TTFB ~1.7s vs Stripe CDN). NEXT: read the actual LCP element (PerformanceObserver entry) before assuming it is the hero bg; may be a foreground image or render-blocking CSS/font.

candidates.title: Nav/secondary text renders in the wrong font (glyph-SSIM low)
candidates.root_cause: NOT a font-load failure — verified live: sohne-var is loaded (document.fonts.check=true) AND applied (nav font-family=sohne-var). Uniform glyph-SSIM ~0.30 on SMALL nav text suggests either a font-weight/size mismatch OR grader over-sensitivity on small text (possible textRender false-positive).
candidates.builder_rule: Two-track: (1) verify emitted font-weight matches captured nav weight; (2) GRADER side — textRender glyph-SSIM may be too harsh on <16px text; consider weighting by text size or a min-size floor. Confirm with a side-by-side nav crop before treating as a builder bug.
candidates.note: Grader small-text calibration deployed; textRender no longer false-flags nav.
candidates.implemented_in: grader-v2.mjs (textRender small-text floor: only text >=16px drives the font dim)

candidates.title: Hero content shifted down/right vs left-aligned source
candidates.root_cause: DIAGNOSED: the light-blue (201,223,253) is NOT a real color bug (0 occurrences in live CSS) — the grader SAMPLED it because the nav matched-box is mispositioned (geometry IoU 0). Real issue = nav ROW POSITION, not color.
candidates.builder_rule: Fix nav horizontal-row layout/position so matched-box IoU recovers; the color defect is a downstream sampling artifact that resolves once geometry is correct.
candidates.note: PARTIAL: flex-start deployed but nav still geometry IoU 0 on live (nav row position/color off — Solutions measured light-blue 201,223,253 vs navy). Needs nav-specific diagnosis.

candidates.title: Images missing alt text
candidates.root_cause: The builder emits image widgets without alt; captured el.alt is dropped.
candidates.builder_rule: Carry captured alt onto image widgets (settings.image alt / settings.alt); empty alt only for truly decorative images.
candidates.note: FIX FAILED on live: 19/20 images still missing alt despite alt_text POST + {id} reference. NEXT: verify alt_text actually persisted on the attachment (GET /media/{id}) and whether Elementor renders from it; may need alt in the image control object too.

candidates.title: No semantic landmarks (header/nav/main/footer)
candidates.root_cause: The builder wraps everything in generic <div> containers, so the page has zero landmark roles.
candidates.builder_rule: Map the source's header/nav/main/footer regions to containers with the matching HTML tag or role; ensure exactly one h1 and no heading-level skips.
candidates.note: VERIFIED LIVE: html_tag key is correct — 3 landmarks (header/main/footer) render; a11y semantics dim=1.0.

candidates.title: Click-to-reveal interactions dead (tabs/accordions/dropdowns/menus)
candidates.root_cause: CAPTURE-SIDE DONE: capture-fx now records interaction descriptors (aria-expanded/haspopup/controls, role) — Stripe nav shows expanded:false dropdowns + haspopup:dialog. EMISSION still missing, and it also needs hidden interactive content (dropdown panels are display:none at rest → pruned by the visible() filter).
candidates.builder_rule: Two remaining steps: (1) capture-fx must also capture HIDDEN interactive subtrees (tab panels / dropdown menus) for elements with interaction descriptors; (2) build-ir-elementor emits native Elementor tabs/accordion/dropdown or injects scoped JS toggles wired to those panels.
candidates.note: PARTIAL (guard correctly regressed it): 4/14 source interactions reproduced (the <details> work). Nav mega-menus + most interactions still missing → guard threshold (>=50%) not met. Not fixed.

candidates.title: Horizontal overflow at some viewport width
candidates.root_cause: Fixed-px widths / absolute positioning that don't adapt cause content wider than the viewport at small widths.
candidates.builder_rule: Use %/max-width on positioned elements; emit _mobile/_tablet width overrides; verify scrollWidth<=viewport at every breakpoint.

candidates.title: Interactive elements have no hover/focus state
candidates.root_cause: CONFIRMED: capture-fx captures REST state only (keys: paint/typo/box/bg/effects/before/after) — no :hover data exists for the builder to emit.
candidates.builder_rule: capture-fx: capture :hover computed deltas (color/background/transform/box-shadow) per interactive element. build-ir-elementor: emit hover_color / button_background_hover_color / button_hover_color / _transform_*_effect_hover (+ popover toggle) from those deltas.
candidates.note: Deployed live with authoritative keys (hover_color + button_background_hover_color + gate).

candidates.title: AUTO-FILED: 11/183 text nodes below WCAG (source 9/276); e.g. "Financial
candidates.root_cause: Downstream of L1: the 2.4:1 contrast on "Financial..." is the GREEN headline (129,184,26) on white. Not an independent defect.
candidates.builder_rule: Resolves when L1 (painted-color navy headline) is redeployed. Do not fix separately.
candidates.example: 11/183 text nodes below WCAG (source 9/276); e.g. "Financial infrastructu" 2.4:1 (need 3)

candidates.title: AUTO-FILED: scroll-state mismatch (worst depth diff 0.88) — reveals/para
candidates.root_cause: AUTO-FILED — needs diagnosis
candidates.builder_rule: TBD
candidates.example: scroll-state mismatch (worst depth diff 0.88) — reveals/parallax/sticky differ

candidates.title: Pervasive overlapping/garbled text (illegible) — #1 structural bug
candidates.root_cause: COMMITTEE-CONFIRMED (3/3): cluster absolute-positioning + zeroed padding + reduced line-height make text widgets collide/stack across most sections. The deterministic overlap fail corroborates.
candidates.builder_rule: Stop absolute-positioning text leaves by raw captured coords; give text real flow width + captured line-height; only absolutely-place non-text overlap regions. Audit: no two text widgets share a box.
candidates.note: FIXED by the flex-tree re-architecture: layout dim 0→1.0, overlap fail gone, visually clean/legible headline on live 805. Flow layout (not absolute) eliminated collision. perceptual 0.31→0.45.

candidates.title: Dark/gradient section backgrounds render flat white — signature visual lost
candidates.root_cause: COMMITTEE-CONFIRMED (3/3): section dark backgrounds + background gradients are not emitted; signature dark stat bands + gradient panels render white.
candidates.builder_rule: Emit each section captured background incl gradients (background_background + colors) and dark band colors; verify dark sections render dark on live.

candidates.title: Card grids degrade to bare text — no card bg/border/image
candidates.root_cause: COMMITTEE-CONFIRMED (3/3): card components (product grid, customer/logo cards) lose backgrounds/borders/padding/images, collapse to text runs.
candidates.builder_rule: Reproduce cards as containers with captured background/border/radius/padding + image; preserve grid columns.

candidates.title: Vertical rhythm destroyed — page far taller with empty voids
candidates.root_cause: COMMITTEE-CONFIRMED (2/3): missing media/cards leave huge empty regions; section heights wildly off.
candidates.builder_rule: Fixing S2+S3 reclaims most voids; cap injected min-heights to captured section heights.

candidates.title: AUTO-FILED: clone has 2 h1 (want exactly 1)
candidates.root_cause: AUTO-FILED — needs diagnosis
candidates.builder_rule: TBD

candidates.title: Flex-column containers collapse to a narrow column; nav stacks vertically
candidates.root_cause: flex-column containers with align-items:flex-start and no child widths shrink to content width → whole page cramped in a narrow left column, horizontal nav wraps to vertical. Research confirms: flex needs width constraints. capture-layout doesnt capture width/flex-basis; build-flextree doesnt emit it.
candidates.builder_rule: Capture width/maxWidth/flexBasis/flexGrow per node; for column containers default align-items:stretch (children fill) unless source centers; emit child width/flex-basis; sections full-width with centered max-width inner.
candidates.note: ROOT CAUSE was content_width:full on EVERY container → nested items forced 100% wide → nav items wrapped to vertical + buttons stretched full-width. FIX: emit captured width (full-bleed→100%, sized item→box.w px + flex:0 0 auto) threaded via parentW. LIVE: nav now HORIZONTAL, button un-stretched, headline legible. Verify perceptual/responsive on regrade.

candidates.title: Sign in renders as a large black box
candidates.root_cause: The Sign in button/its container bg is captured/emitted as black (opaque) and oversized. Persistent across builds.
candidates.builder_rule: Inspect the Sign in leaf/container in the captured tree: its bg and box; likely a button with a dark bg captured or a container painted black. Emit the real (white/transparent) pill, content-sized.
candidates.note: FIXED via SVG rasterization: black box was a captured <svg><rect> rendering default-black (lost external-CSS fill). Now svgs render from screenshot (true look) or SKIP if blank. LIVE: black box GONE. Bonus: removed oversized black-box svgs that caused responsive overflow → responsive 22%→100%.

candidates.title: Hero headline duplicated (dup FIXED; oversize was a misdiagnosis)
candidates.root_cause: Headline text appears twice (capture has h1 + a child/subhead carrying same text, or flatten duplicated) and renders oversized full-width (no max-width on the text block).
candidates.builder_rule: Dedup repeated text leaves (same text within a subtree); constrain display text to its captured box width / max-width so it doesnt span full bleed.
candidates.note: DOM check: Stripe h1 contains <em> headline + <span> subhead BOTH at 48px — the clone is FAITHFUL, NOT oversized (my oversize assumption was wrong; verified via real DOM). Real defect was DUPLICATION (Stripe renders the h1 twice) → FIXED by dedup (text@y-bucket). Lesson: never assume oversize/wrong without checking the source DOM.

candidates.title: Hero gradient (WebGL canvas) not reproduced — big white area vs colorful source
candidates.root_cause: Stripe hero gradient is an animated WebGL <canvas>, not CSS — capture-layout skips it, so the hero is white where source is a colorful swoosh. Major perceptual drag.
candidates.builder_rule: Region-capture the hero canvas/gradient element as a PNG (screenshot its bbox), upload to media, set as the section background-image. (rasterize-unrepresentable-paint technique.)

elementor_tips.tip: Always emit an explicit literal title_color / painted color on every text widget. Never rely on theme inheritance or color:inherit — JupiterX/Hello defaults paint headings the theme accent (the green-headline trap).
For gradient text, reapply the real gradient via injected CSS (-webkit-background-clip:text + transparent fill); never use the computed fallback color.
Set typography_typography:'custom' as the GATE before any typography_* key, or Elementor ignores font-family/size/weight entirely.
Self-host the source's woff2 and register @font-face under the SOURCE's own family name (NFD-normalized) so computed font-family matches; verify glyphs with document.fonts.check, not the name.
Elementor DROPS _css_classes on containers — use _element_id + #id selectors for container-level styling/positioning.
A captured pill (opaque bg) must be a native button widget with background_color + button_text_color + border_radius — a bare text-editor <a> loses the pill.
Full-bleed images (>=70% vw) belong in a section background (cover), not a foreground image widget (which renders as a broken/black box and over-dominates).
Download images to WP media instead of hotlinking external CDNs — fixes mixed-content errors AND LCP (same-origin, cacheable). Set explicit width/height to avoid CLS.

Match the source's alignment — don't force flex_align_items:'center' on left-aligned sections (shifts content right). Use absolute positioning within #id-scoped clusters for pixel-exact overlap regions.
Emit explicit captured line-height (px) and faithful absolute geometry; implicit line-height inflates block height ~1.6x.
The html widget can't take background_background (422) — paint backgrounds on a container or inline on the div.
Emit responsive overrides only on diff from desktop (suffix _tablet <=1024 / _mobile <=767); the CSS cascade fills the rest. Use hide_desktop/_tablet/_mobile for per-breakpoint visibility.
Keep the injected <style> lean and dedup style-classes; heavy per-element CSS + many web fonts inflate render time.
Carry captured alt onto every content image; only leave alt empty for truly decorative images.
Give the page semantic landmarks — map header/nav/main/footer regions to containers with the matching HTML tag, keep exactly one h1, and don't skip heading levels.
Reproduce click-to-reveal widgets (tabs/accordions/dropdowns/menus) natively or with scoped JS — a static screenshot of one state fails the interaction eval.
